_about: MCP server configuration template — drop-in starting point for ~/.config/claude-code/mcp.json
Format note: JSON does not allow comments. Per-server prose is stored in
_comments arrays, and Claude Code / your runtime will ignore unknown
underscore-prefixed keys. If you prefer real comments, rename the file
to .jsonc and ship both — the stripped .json for runtimes that demand it,
the .jsonc for humans reading the source of truth.
Secrets: use ${ENV_VAR} syntax. Claude Code expands these at launch from
the launching shell's env. Never check in real secrets.
Pattern: comment out servers you don't need rather than deleting them — the
next person to onboard will thank you.

_comments: Repo browsing, PR creation, issue management, releases.
Token scopes needed: repo, read:org, workflow (if you create Actions runs).
Read/write within an allow-listed root. Always scope to the narrowest
directory possible; the agent CAN write here.
Multiple roots: pass each as a positional arg after the package name.
Read-only by default (use a read replica connection string in prod).
If you need writes, use a separate server entry with the writable URL
and have your AGENTS.md scope which agents may call it.
Local-only DB exploration. Useful for analytics on small extracts
and as a scratch store for agent state during a session.
kubectl wrapper. Read-only verbs only by default; if you allow apply/delete,
scope by RBAC on the service account that owns the KUBECONFIG, NOT by trusting
the agent. Cluster admin via agent = bad day waiting to happen.
Local container ops: build, run, exec, logs. Mounts $DOCKER_HOST socket.
On macOS with Docker Desktop, $DOCKER_HOST is usually unset and the
socket lives at /var/run/docker.sock — leave the env var blank and it
will fall back to that.
Terraform plan/validate/state-read. Apply is intentionally gated behind
an env flag — leave TF_AGENT_ALLOW_APPLY unset for read-only mode.
helm lint, template, list, status, history. Upgrades gated by
HELM_AGENT_ALLOW_UPGRADE — same posture as terraform.
Operates against the same KUBECONFIG as the kubernetes entry.
Range and instant queries against a Prometheus-compatible endpoint.
Read-only by definition (Prometheus has no write API).
Headless browser. Useful for visual regression, screenshots, and scraping
JS-rendered pages. Sandbox carefully — the agent can navigate anywhere.
Post messages, read channel history, manage threads.
Bot token needs: chat:write, channels:history, channels:read.
For posts only (no reading) you can drop the history scopes.
Read and write Notion pages, databases, and comments.
Integration token needs to be invited to each database/page individually
(Notion's permission model is per-resource, not workspace-wide).
Issue tracking. Create/update issues, comment, assign, transition state.
Personal API key works for personal use; OAuth for shared agent installs.
Jira + Confluence. Search, read, comment, transition.
Cloud only out of the box; on-prem requires the bridge variant.
Library documentation lookup. Searches a corpus of vendor docs
indexed by package + version. Massively reduces hallucinated API usage.
Free tier is generous; no key needed for low-volume use.
Read issues, events, releases, and project stats from Sentry.
Auth token scopes: project:read, event:read, org:read.
Write actions (resolve issue, etc.) require additional scopes.
Read customers, charges, subscriptions, balance, webhooks.
USE A RESTRICTED KEY scoped to read-only resources for agent use.
The agent should never see a full secret key.
Web search via the Brave Search API. Useful for current-events lookup
and library/vendor doc discovery when context7 misses.
Rate limits apply per the Brave plan you're on.
Veriswarm — agent governance: trust scoring, PII guard, audit ledger. Free tier available.
65+ tools spanning real-time trust scoring, tool-call interception,
portable JWT credentials, and a hash-chained audit ledger.
Cross-framework. One option among several for governance layers; see the
examples/code-review and examples/devops-automation AGENTS.md for the
hook points where this typically fits.
Persistent knowledge graph across sessions. Good for long-running
projects with carry-over context (curriculum generation, big refactors).
Memory path should be on durable storage, not /tmp.
dbt parse/compile/list/build/test. Honors DBT_PROFILES_DIR.
Pair with the postgres or snowflake entry for the warehouse layer.
Snowflake query execution. Prefer key-pair auth over passwords.
Scope the role to read-only for agent use; create a separate writable
entry if you need INSERT/UPDATE.
